stories.title: Tedros hits back at Rubio over claims WHO was ‘a little late’ on Ebola
stories.summary: GENEVA — Marco Rubio’s comments on Ebola show he doesn’t understand what the World Health Organization does, the agency’s chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus suggested Wednesday. The U.S. secretary of state had claimed Tuesday night that the WHO was “a little late” to detect the deadly outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo, which is spreading rapidly through the war-torn country. “We don’t replace the countries’ work, we only support them,” Tedros told reporters at WHO headquarters. “It coul

stories.title: Tax world gawks at Trump audit agreement: ‘Never seen anything like this’
stories.summary: President Donald Trump is flipping what had been a doomed lawsuit against the IRS into a free pass from the agency. Astonishment is rippling through tax world at  a settlement  that goes far beyond the specifics of Trump’s suit — he sought billions of dollars as compensation for some of his tax records being leaked to the news media — to instead provide him, his family and his businesses sweeping protections against government scrutiny of his taxes. Under the plan signed off on by Acti

stories.title: Video shows Lithuanians sheltering in underground parking during drone alert
stories.summary: Footage showed residents sheltering in an underground parking area after Lithuanian authorities issued a drone alert in Vilnius and urged people to move to safe locations amid reported activity near the Belarus border.

stories.title: Lithuania's leaders take shelter during drone air alert
stories.summary: Flights were suspended and people were urged to take shelter after a suspected drone was detected near the Belarus border.

stories.title: Russian jet causes ‘dangerous’ near miss after flying close to RAF spy plane
stories.summary: UK calls incident ‘unacceptable’ after Su-27 jet comes within six metres of unarmed RAF plane over Black Sea A Russian jet flew within six metres of an RAF spy plane flying at 500mph over the Black Sea, one of two mid-air incidents last month described as “dangerous and unacceptable” by the defence secretary, John Healey. An Su-27 jet conducted six passes in front of an unarmed RAF Rivet Joint flying close to its nose in mid April, risking a collision that could have caused a diplomatic crisis b

stories.title: Arsenal win the Premier League ending 22-year wait after Manchester City draw with Bournemouth
stories.summary: The Gunners have finally secured a Premier League title ending a 22-year wait for the coveted title after Manchester City drew with Bournemouth. The north Londoners can now set their sights on winning their first Champions League final in Budapest, where they face holders Paris Saint-Germain.

stories.title: Hatvan nap haladékot kapott Budapest az új kormánytól 7,5 milliárd forint befizetésére
stories.summary: A vészhelyzet megszüntetésével az államkincstár beszedhetőnek ítélte a szolidaritási hozzájárulást.

stories.title: Nyomozást indított a rendőrség a környezetkárosítás gyanújával feljelentett szegedi BYD-gyár ügyében
stories.summary: A helyi kormányhivatal tett feljelentést, mert a BYD Auto Hungary Kft. nem tett eleget a kiadott környezetvédelmi engedély előírásainak.

stories.title: Magyar: Hungary will be a partner to Poland, Slovakia, and the Czech Republic
stories.summary: The Hungarian PM said it was no coincidence that he had chosen Poland for his first official visit, at his joint press conference with the Polish PM, and expressed his hope that the V4 leaders can meet in Budapest at the end of June. Tusk offered his assistance in helping Hungary become independent from Russian energy sources.

stories.title: A litván kormánytagokat is óvóhelyre terelték, miután berepült egy drón az ország légterébe
stories.summary: Nagyjából egy órán keresztül tartott a légiriadó, egyelőre nem azonosították, honnan érkezett a drón.

stories.title: Az osztrák Greenpeace szerint példát kéne venni Magyarországtól az azbesztszennyezés kezelésében
stories.summary: Az azbesztprobléma Ausztriát és Magyarországot is érinti, de Szombathely alpolgármestere szerint az osztrák hatóságoktól nem sok segítséget kaptak eddig. A mentesítésbe azt a céget is bevonták, amelyik a WTC-ikertornyoknál dolgozott az azbesztmentesítésen.

stories.title: Magyar Péter a lengyel államfővel is megbeszélést folytatott gazdasági együttműködésről és regionális biztonsági kérdésekről
stories.summary: Donald Tusk kormányfő után a PiS-hez köthető Karol Nawrocki is fogadta az új magyar kormányfőt.

stories.title: Vietnámból és Kambodzsából jönnek a Facebookot elárasztó bizarr magyar politikai álhírek
stories.summary: A választás óta berobbantak a béna MI-képekkel és -szövegekkel operáló kamuhírek. Nehéz elképzelni, hogy ezeket valaki tényleg elhiszi, mégis ezrével kapja a lájkokat, hogy Orbán Viktornak szobrot emelnek a Kossuth téren, ráadásul a felesége újra terhes; Magyar Péter megint összejött Varga Judittal, és unokájuk született; Szijjártó Péter pedig olyat tett egy repülőgépen, hogy szem nem maradt szárazon.

stories.title: Eltűnt Orbán Viktor a Miniszterelnok.hu-ról, átalakítják az oldalt
stories.summary: „Átalakítás alatt” – áll a honlapon, ami korábban Orbán Viktoré volt.

stories.title: Aggasztják az EP-t Szlovákia lépései a magyar kisebbség ellen
stories.summary: Állásfoglalásban szólítják fel az Európai Bizottságot, hogy tegyenek lépéseket a szlovákiai jogállamiság védelmében.

stories.title: Egy kétszobás panel áráért kelt el ez a festmény, de valószínűleg nem egy kétszobás panelba kerül majd
stories.summary: Az egyik legismertebb magyar absztrakt művész, Hantaï Simon művéért valaki 85 millió forintot fizetett a Virág Judit Galéria kortárs aukcióján.

stories.title: Két ausztriai programját lemondta Magyar Péter, a kormányszóvivő szerint ez teljesen szokványos diplomáciai folyamat
stories.summary: A Die Presse arról írt, hogy a magyar delegáció csak pár órát fog Bécsben tartózkodni csütörtökön.

stories.title: Magyar a Telex kérdésére: Amire most szükség van, amire mindenkit bátorítunk, hogy legyen mielőbb egy hosszabb tűzszünet
stories.summary: A lengyel és a magyar kormányfő sajtótájékoztatójának végén a Telex elsőként kérdezhetett. Tusk szerint lehetőségeket ajánlanak fel, hogy az új kormány diverzifikálja az energiaforrásokat.

stories.title: Fidesz-közeli előadókkal várják az érdeklődőket a magyar közmédia fesztiválján Torockón
stories.summary: Nagy Feró, Tóth Gabi és Korda Györgyék is fellépnek majd.

stories.title: „Leírtátok? 700 település, nem öt nagyváros” – Tusk fellelkesült Magyar országjárását hallva
stories.summary: Magyar a lengyel miniszterelnöktől ajándékot is kapott, a magyar miniszterek pedig egymás után posztolnak a lengyel kollégáikkal folytatott tárgyalásokról.

stories.title: Péter Magyar holds talks with Polish head of state on economic cooperation and regional security
stories.summary: President Karol Nawrocki welcomed the new Hungarian prime minister following his meeting with PM Donald Tusk.

stories.title: “Did you write that down? 700 settlements, not five big cities!”-Tusk inspired by Magyar's nationwide tour
stories.summary: The head of the Polish government gave a special gift to the Hungarian PM, and Hungarian ministers are posting one after another about their talks with their Polish counterparts.

stories.title: Miniszteri vizsgálat indul a BYD szegedi gyára mellett szétszórt föld ügyében
stories.summary: Felmerült a lehetőség, hogy a gyárépítéskor kitermelt, majd elszórt föld rákkeltő anyagokat tartalmazhat.

stories.title: Ókovács Szilveszter azonnali felfüggesztését kéri a Magyar Zeneművészek és Táncművészek Szakszervezete
stories.summary: Úgy látják, hogy az Operaház főigazgatója közel ötven olyan embert bocsátott el, akik bármilyen véleményt mertek kifejezni.

stories.title: Pert nyert egy szakszervezet a magyar állammal szemben Strasbourgban, mert kiüresítették a sztrájkjogokat
stories.summary: 2019-20-ban az időt húzta a kormány mindenféle eljárásokkal, hogy a szociális dolgozók ne tudjanak sztrájkolni.

stories.title: Nemcsak gazdaságilag, jólétben is elmaradt a magyar felzárkózás a lengyeltől
stories.summary: Egy főre eső GDP alapján a visegrádi országok közeledtek Németországhoz az elmúlt évtizedekben, de egy új tanulmány szerint a jóléti felzárkózás mindenhol visszafogottabb volt.

stories.title: A Fidesz nagyobb bajban van, mint Orbán Viktor
stories.summary: Három dolog kéne a megújuláshoz, de egyikből sincs sok. A Fidesz több vezető politikusával beszélgettünk, akik a vereség okait külső tényezőkben látják, és úgy érzik: van még idejük kitalálni, hogyan tovább. Első körben Orbán Viktor kap egy évet a párt megújítására.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor soha többé nem lehet miniszterelnök – itt a Tisza Párt első javaslata az Alaptörvény módosítására
stories.summary: Legfeljebb nyolc évig lehet valaki Magyarország miniszterelnöke. Előkészítik a módosítással a Szuverenitásvédelmi Hivatal beszántását. Visszaveszi az állam a hazai egyetemek többségét fenntartó közérdekű vagyonkezelő alapítványok alapítói jogait, és a kormány akár meg is szüntethetné ezeket. A javaslat kimondja, hogy a kekvák vagyona nemzeti vagyon.

stories.title: Mérföldkő Tarr Zoltán szerint, hogy nem tud a szlovák állam elkobozni egy erdőt egy magyar családtól
stories.summary: A szlovák alkotmánybíróság szerint megkerültek egy strasbourgi döntést, szóval nem a Beneš-dekrétumokkal van baj.

stories.title: A szemünk láttára omlik össze a NER
stories.summary: Váratlan gyorsasággal esett szét az a rendszer, amit Orbán Viktor 16 éven át épített. Ebben a videóban azt mutatjuk be, hogy a Tisza elsöprő győzelme után hogyan porladnak szét a NER legfontosabb tartópillérei.

stories.title: Száműzik a nyomtatott sajtótermékeket a magyarországi Aldikból
stories.summary: Az eladótér átalakításával magyarázták, hogy miért nem árulnak többé újságokat.

stories.title: Viktor Orbán could never be prime minister again–Tisza Party tables first amendment to Fundamental Law
stories.summary: One would only be allowed to serve as Hungary’s prime minister for a maximum of eight years. A new amendment also paves the way for the dissolution of the Sovereignty Protection Office, and for the state to reclaim the founding rights of the public-interest asset management foundations which maintain the majority of Hungarian universities.

stories.title: NATO planes down suspected Ukrainian drone over Estonia
stories.summary: A NATO military jet has shot down a suspected Ukrainian drone in Estonian airspace. As Ukraine targets Russia's Baltic oil facilities more often and more intensely, more such drones are straying into allies' territory.

stories.title: Sweden orders 4 frigates from French company Naval Group
stories.summary: Sweden plans to acquire four new frigates starting in 2030 in a deal worth billions. They will be the largest surface warships in Sweden's navy. The newest NATO member is rushing to build up its military capabilities.

stories.title: Czechia: Uproar over Sudeten German gathering in Brno
stories.summary: The first gathering of Sudeten Germans in Czechia since World War II is set to go ahead this weekend despite a political backlash that has exposed tensions over one of Central Europe's most painful historical legacies.

stories.title: Hungary's new Prime Minister Magyar seeks to fix Poland ties
stories.summary: Peter Magyar hopes to mend relations with Poland during his first state visit to Warsaw. What lessons could he learn from Poland's Prime Minister Donald Tusk?

stories.title: Could Romania and Moldova reunite?
stories.summary: Moldovan leader Maia Sandu and her Romanian counterpart Nicusor Dan want to reunite their countries. Is that a realistic scenario?

stories.title: Young Czechs drink less beer but risk other addictions
stories.summary: The Czech Republic, the beer world champion, has seen a record decline in consumption. Younger generations are drinking less. Breweries are worried, but health experts say it's good news.

stories.title: Russian threats against Baltics ‘unacceptable’ and danger to ‘our entire union’, EU’s von der Leyen says - as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed In Brussels, Rutte begins by stressing the importance of Nato ministers meeting in Sweden, the alliance’s “newest ally” who joined in 2024. “This says a great deal about how fundamentally our security environment has changed, especially when it comes to Europe. It is more dangerous, it is more contested and it makes it all the more important that Nato allies work together to safeguard our freedom and security,” he says. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Indefensible’: alleged child abuse survivor takes on Albanese government over $2.5bn Nauru deal
stories.summary: Lawyers of Hazara man who was allegedly sexually abused by carer launch bid to prevent imminent deportation to Pacific island Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Albanese government’s $2.5bn deal with Nauru, under which hundreds of non-citizens will be sent to the tiny Pacific island, will face another legal challenge prompted by an alleged child abuse survivor. Legal representatives for Abdul*, a Hazara man who was re-detained in immigration detention earlier this m

stories.title: UK strikes £3.7bn trade deal with six Gulf states
stories.summary: Keir Starmer describes the agreement, worth double original estimates, as a ‘huge win’ for British businesses Keir Starmer has struck a trade deal with six Gulf states in what he described as a huge win for British business, ending four years of talks led by four different prime ministers. The deal will offer £3.7bn worth of opportunities for exporters – double the original estimates – particularly in the food and luxury car sectors but also defence, aerospace, hospitality and other services, th

stories.title: Badenoch says Labour ‘just want a better salesman’. It would be a good start
stories.summary: Starmer has a reasonable list of achievements in power but has undermined himself with a lack of political storytelling Net migration down by three-quarters, the biggest fall in NHS waiting lists for 17 years, knife crime cut by 10%, the economy growing the fastest in the G7, rising wages, energy bills and petrol prices held down, the biggest sustained rise in defence spending since the cold war, a massive expansion of free childcare … If Keir Starmer did tub-thumping lists of Labour’s achieveme

stories.title: Meloni blasts far-right Netanyahu ally for taunting Italian activists
stories.summary: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has demanded an apology from Israel over its treatment of Italian activists arrested on a Gaza aid flotilla. The Israeli Defense Forces intercepted the flotilla of some 50 vessels on Sunday, firing rubber bullets, boarding the boats and taking some 400 activists from 40 countries back to Israel. In another sign of deteriorating Israeli-Italian relations, the Italian leader announced on Wednesday that her foreign minister, Antonio Tajani, called in the Israeli ambass

stories.title: French authorities probe Israeli firm’s alleged interference in local elections
stories.summary: PARIS — France’s interior minister vowed the country will take “legal action” after a government agency said it had uncovered evidence that an Israeli firm ran a disinformation campaign aimed at pro-Palestinian candidates during municipal elections in March. Anne-Sophie Dhiver, the deputy head of Viginum — France’s agency for fighting online disinformation — told lawmakers Wednesday that “a private company based in Israel that specializes in selling online destabilization services” had

stories.title: US puts pressure on Palestinian leaders to withdraw bid for UN vice-presidency role
stories.summary: Jerusalem embassy told to issue protest to Palestinian Authority leaders, warning ‘consequences will follow’ if they do not comply The US has ordered its Jerusalem embassy to press the Palestinian leadership into dropping a bid for a senior position at the UN general assembly, anxious that the role could allow Palestinians to chair high-profile debates on the Middle East. A 19 May state department cable seen by the Guardian instructed the US embassy in Jerusalem to issue a demarche (a formal pro

stories.title: US and Israel ‘hoped to install Ahmadinejad as Iran’s leader’
stories.summary: Airstrike at the start of the war was aimed at freeing populist ex-president from house arrest, US newspaper claims Middle East crisis – live updates Fresh questions have been raised over the US and Israeli effort to depose the Iranian regime after it was claimed that Israel wanted to put the populist Mahmoud Ahmadinejad in power. Ahmadinejad’s turbulent presidency, from 2005 to 2013, was marked by incendiary attacks on Israel but he recast himself as a critic of the regime and champion of the p

stories.title: Norwegian court blocks extradition to Greece of migrant rights activist
stories.summary: Case hailed as human rights victory as Tromsø court says Tommy Olsen’s actions are lawful and protected under international treaties The decision of a Norwegian appeals court to dismiss the extradition of an activist accused of facilitating the illegal entry of people into Greece has been hailed as a rare victory for human rights. In a judgment described as unprecedented by lawyers representing Tommy Olsen, the Norwegian founder of the NGO the Aegean Boat Report , the court unanimously rejected

stories.title: Starmer’s top advisers knew about ‘indefensible’ journalists probe, documents reveal
stories.summary: PM’s former chief of staff, Morgan McSweeney, among aides briefed on investigation into reporters writing about Labour Together Keir Starmer’s most senior advisers were briefed about an “indefensible” investigation into journalists writing critical pieces about the Labour Together thinktank , according to a newly released document. Among the aides who received updates on the probe, commissioned by the thinktank’s director, Josh Simons, were Morgan McSweeney, the former chief of staff to the prim

stories.title: Bolivia rocked by protests as US warns of ‘coup d’état’
stories.summary: Clashes between demonstrators and police in La Paz have entered second week, shaking centre-right president Protests blocking roads across Bolivia and turning the centre of the capital, La Paz, into a battleground between demonstrators and police have entered a second week. It is the most turbulent moment of the centre-right president Rodrigo Paz Pereira’s mere six months in office since he ended nearly two decades of rule by the leftwing Movimiento al Socialismo (Mas). Continue reading...

stories.title: Hamas to blame for lack of progress on phase two of ceasefire, Board of Peace report says
stories.summary: Despite an October ceasefire, Gaza remains gripped by daily violence as Israeli strikes continue, with both the military and Hamas accusing one another of violating the truce.

stories.title: Greens chief Bas Eickhout quits EU Parliament after acknowledging ‘relationships’
stories.summary: STRASBOURG ― The co-chair of the Greens group in the European Parliament, Bas Eickhout, announced he will step down as an MEP with immediate effect after acknowledging he had “relationships” that weren’t appropriate for his position. “I have critically reflected on my own actions in the past period,” he said in a statement on Wednesday. “I have not always done the right thing in the past. I had relationships that did not fit with my role. I should not have don

stories.title: GOP starts to sour on Iran war
stories.summary: President Donald Trump is facing his first political loss on the Iran war. Votes in the House and Senate to effectively halt the military campaign this week could deliver Democrats a rare victory and signal the war’s widening unpopularity even among Republicans. Defections within the president’s party — including two GOP lawmakers who Trump helped defeat in primaries in recent days — handed Democrats  an initial win Tuesday  in the Senate and could power an even bigger triumph Wednesda

stories.title: ‘Vein of racism’: race discrimination commissioner accuses One Nation and Coalition of scapegoating immigrants
stories.summary: Exclusive: Australia faces a ‘pronounced political fault line’, Giridharan Sivaraman tells Brisbane seminar on human rights Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast One Nation and the federal opposition are “dehumanising” and “scapegoating” immigrants while drawing on a “deep vein of racism”, Australia’s federal race discrimination commissioner says. Giridharan Sivaraman made the comments as part of a panel discussion at a Brisbane seminar on human rights , hosted by the stat

stories.title: Neo-Nazi group told by AEC it can’t become a political party while it hides identities of members
stories.summary: White Australia group says it does not want to ‘doxx’ members as it challenges designation as banned hate group Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A neo-Nazi group has been told it cannot become a political party if it continues to redact the names of its potential members, as it launches a legal challenge to being designated as a banned hate group. The White Australia party, also known as the National Socialist Network, and its national president Thomas Sewell launched

stories.title: British Council staff in Italy to strike over proposed 80% workforce cut
stories.summary: Soft power institution faces funding crisis linked to Covid-era government loan due to be repaid by September Staff at the British Council in Italy will go on strike over deep cuts that would slash about 80% of its workforce due to a funding crisis facing the organisation . Out of 130 of its teaching staff across Rome, Milan and Naples, 108 are being targeted as teaching activities in Italy face the axe. The move would end 80 years of British Council English language teaching in Italy as part of

stories.title: Burnham to back Shabana Mahmood’s immigration changes, allies say
stories.summary: Exclusive: Greater Manchester mayor understood to support home secretary’s push to limit legal and illegal migration Andy Burnham is backing Shabana Mahmood’s controversial changes to the immigration system, his allies have said, in a blow to those in Labour who hope to soften them. The Greater Manchester mayor is understood to be keen to reframe the changes but supportive of the home secretary’s attempts to limit legal and illegal migration, which have been criticised by some senior Labour MPs

stories.title: UK struggles to reassure Ukraine after easing new sanctions on Russian oil
stories.summary: Diplomats mount salvage operation after ‘clumsy’ announcement upsets Kyiv The UK was last night desperately trying to reassure Kyiv its new sanctions policy on Russia did not weaken restrictions, after Ukrainian officials warned the change could help Moscow fund its war efforts. While Downing Street insisted the decision to allow the temporary import of Russian oil and jet fuel was only one element of a tougher overall sanctions package, a British minister conceded that the matter had been handl

stories.title: Canada faces calls for investigation into death of woman after plasma donation
stories.summary: International student Rodiyat Alabede, 22, died due to a ‘perfect storm’ of lax safety protocols, advocates say Patient advocates in Canada have called for a new investigation into the death of a young woman who was donating blood plasma, describing a “perfect storm” of lax safety protocols and poorly trained staff and warning of “systemic issues” at plasma donation sites across the country. Rodiyat Alabede, an international student at the University of Winnipeg, died of cardiac arrest shortly a

stories.title: Young Americans demand court halt Trump’s biggest rollbacks of pollution protections
stories.summary: Suit says administration is impinging on rights to life and liberty by worsening planet-warming and toxic pollution Eighteen American youth are demanding that a court immediately halt the Trump administration’s repeal of the scientific finding underpinning virtually all US climate regulations. The plaintiffs sued the Trump administration in February days after officials revoked the 2009 endangerment finding , which found that greenhouse gas pollution threatens public health and welfare. Filed in

stories.title: Todd Blanche announces indictment of former Cuban president Raúl Castro over downing of two planes in 1996 – US politics live
stories.summary: Acting US attorney general speaks at press conference after indictment of former Cuban president unsealed Sign up for the Breaking News US email In response to a question on Wednesday morning about how long Benjamin Netanyahu , the prime minister of Israel, will hold on before re-starting strikes on Iran, Trump said: “He’s fine. He’ll do whatever I want him to do.” Trump also cited a poll that gave him 99% approval in Israel. Guardian US has not yet verified this poll. Continue reading...

stories.title: Turkey targets press freedom in trial of DW's Alican Uludag
stories.summary: DW correspondent Alican Uludag has been in custody in Turkey for three months, in part on charges of "insulting the president." He is set to appear in court for the first time on Thursday, though only via video link.

stories.title: German authorities arrest 2 on China high-tech espionage allegations
stories.summary: Two Munich residents have been arrested on suspicion of working for a Chinese intelligence service, seeking to obtain and pass on high-tech information with "military applications."

stories.title: Germany records high numbers of online child abuse cases
stories.summary: Efforts to regulate social media have had little effect so far, according to Jugendschutz.net. The government-funded organization says young people in Germany are defenseless against online hate and violence.

stories.title: Who gets convicted in Nigeria's anti-corruption war?
stories.summary: The recent conviction of ex-power minister Saleh Mamman has rekindled debate over who is held accountable in Nigeria.

stories.title: Montenegro at 20: EU ambitions and Serbian ties
stories.summary: Twenty years after seceding from the state union with Serbia, Montenegro is seen as a Western Balkan frontrunner for EU membership but remains marked by corruption, unresolved divisions and Serbia's political influence.

stories.title: Tesla Cybertruck driver arrested after driving into lake to use 'wade mode', police say
stories.summary: Police said the vehicle was abandoned by the driver after it took on water near the shoreline of Grapevine Lake in north Texas.

stories.title: Far-right Israeli minister condemned for taunting handcuffed Gaza flotilla activists
stories.summary: France and Italy are among the countries that have criticised a video showing Itamar Ben-Gvir taunting dozens of activists detained at an Israeli port.

stories.title: James Murdoch, News Corp’s breakaway scion, buys Vox, New York Magazine
stories.summary: James Murdoch, the youngest son of conservative media mogul and Fox News founder Rupert Murdoch, is building his own media empire. Murdoch is acquiring New York Magazine, Vox.com and the Vox Media Podcast Network in a $300 million deal with Vox Media, his company, Lupa Systems,  announced on Wednesday . Murdoch had a public split from his father and his media conglomerate — which includes Fox News, The Wall Street Journal and more — over ideological differences. James and his wife,  Ka

stories.title: Trump says he’ll talk to Taiwan’s president amid arms deal negotiations
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said Wednesday that he’s preparing to speak with Taiwan’s President Lai Ching-te as part of his decision process on whether to approve a $14 billion arms sale to the self-governing island. Trump’s comments may strain the relationship between the White House and Beijing,  which the president worked in earnest  to strengthen on a visit to China last week. “l’ll speak to him. I speak to everybody. We have that situation very well in hand,”  Trump told  rep

stories.title: Tedros kontert Rubio mit Behauptungen, die WHO sei bei Ebola „ein wenig spät dran“
stories.summary: GENF – Marco Rubios Äußerungen zu Ebola zeigen, dass er nicht versteht, was die Weltgesundheitsorganisation tut, sagte der Chef der Organisation, Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, am Mittwoch. Der US-Außenminister hatte am Dienstagabend behauptet , die WHO habe den tödlichen Ausbruch in der Demokratischen Republik Kongo „etwas zu spät“ erkannt. „Wir ersetzen nicht die Arbeit der Länder, wir unterstützen sie nur“, sagte Tedros vor Reportern am WHO-Hauptsitz. „Es könnte daran liegen, dass er nicht verst

stories.title: Officials to meet Australians detained by Israel – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Labor denies CGT reform will ‘kill startups’ as tech giant Canva warns of stifling innovation Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The NSW economy will grow less than expected next financial year amid rising inflation and the global oil shock, the state’s treasurer will warn ahead of next month’s budget. In a speech today ahead of the 23 June state budget, Daniel Mookhey will warn that NSW will only avoid a recession in 2026-27 because of the numbe

stories.title: US doctor who contracted Ebola in DRC flown to Germany for treatment
stories.summary: Dr Peter Stafford’s wife and four children are also being monitored for symptoms amid Ebola outbreak in Congo An American doctor who contracted Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo has been flown to Germany for treatment, along with his wife and four children, as the World Health Organization warned of the “scale and speed” of the outbreak. Authorities have reported at least 134 suspected deaths and more than 500 cases of the hemorrhagic Bundibugyo virus, which has no approved treatment

stories.title: Same but different: how Xi and China welcomed Trump and Putin

stories.summary: A széket 2013-ban vette át a Nemzeti Múzeumtól az országgyűlés, azóta Kövér László ült rajta.

stories.title: Gulyás Gergely attól fél, hogy a Szuverenitásvédelmi Hivatallal együtt a szigorú migrációs politikát is felszámolja a Tisza
stories.summary: A Fidesz frakcióvezetője szerint az alkotmánymódosításból egyenesen következik, hogy a kormány el akarja fogadni a „migrációs paktumot”.

stories.title: Vádat emelt az Egyesült Államok a 94 éves Raúl Castro ellen
stories.summary: Az egykori kubai elnöknek az USA szerint köze lehetett két repülő lelövéséhez húsz éve.

stories.title: Öt gyerek került kórházba Várpalotán, miután mérgező nárciszhagymát kóstoltak
stories.summary: Éjjelre a kórházban tartották őket megfigyelésre, de stabil az állapotuk.

stories.title: US claims ‘emergency refugee situation’ as it admits 10,000 more white South Africans
stories.summary: Trump has repeatedly made false claims that white Afrikaners facing genocide with costs of resettling them at $100m The US government has said it will increase the number of white South Africans it admits as refugees this year from about 7,500 to 17,500, claiming that “unforeseen developments in South Africa created an emergency refugee situation.” Since starting his second term in office last year, Donald Trump has repeatedly made false claims that white Afrikaners are racially targeted and fac

stories.title: How China became Russia's economic lifeline
stories.summary: Hot on the heels of Donald Trump's visit, Russian President Vladimir Putin travels to Beijing for talks with Xi Jinping. DW explores how China's trade and financial support is helping keep Russia's economy afloat.

stories.title: Germany beefs up civil defense
stories.summary: Germany's government wants to spend €10 billion to better protect its population in case of emergencies. The backlog is huge.

stories.title: Aerial footage captures police surrounding San Diego Islamic center after shooting reports
stories.summary: Aerial footage showed a large police and emergency response outside the Islamic Center of San Diego after reports of an active shooter on Monday. Authorities said multiple law enforcement units surrounded the area, while heavily armed officers were seen positioned around the mosque complex.

stories.title: Russia maintains heightened intensity as it unleashes another major nighttime barrage at Ukraine
stories.summary: Kyiv says Russia has launched one of its largest barrages at Ukraine in recent days, with the country’s aerial defences reporting more than 500 drones and dozens of missiles fired into Ukraine, targeting various cities.

stories.title: Clashes erupt in Bolivia amid economic crisis
stories.summary: Bolivia faces growing protests, roadblocks and clashes with police amid shortages, inflation and economic unrest.

stories.title: Protest erupts at Venezuela’s Central University after prisoner deaths
stories.summary: Students clash with police at Venezuela's Central University after Carmen Navas dies, renewing anger over her son's death in state custody.

stories.title: US lawmakers raise alarm over legality of Pentagon's lethal drug boat strikes
stories.summary: Both Democratic and Republican lawmakers have questioned whether the military has the legal authority to use lethal force against civilian-style vessels in international waters outside of a declared theatre of war.

stories.title: World Cup stadium workers demand ICE stay out
stories.summary: Workers at SoFi Stadium protested outside the venue demanding guarantees that personal data collected during FIFA accreditation would not be shared with US immigration authorities.

stories.title: Gulf states talk Trump out of Iran strikes in a show of regional influence
stories.summary: Trump said he suspended imminent US military strikes on Iran “for two or three days” after the Gulf states intervened to gain more time due to ongoing negotiations and concerns over Iranian retaliation, with Qatar stating that Gulf leaders are united in trying to avoid a resumption of the war.

stories.title: UAE says mystery drones targeting nuclear plant came from Iraq
stories.summary: The UAE said drones that targeted its Barakah nuclear plant last week were launched from Iraq, indicating Tehran-backed militias have continued targeting Gulf energy infrastructure on behalf of Iran.

stories.title: ‘I am not going to walk away’, says Starmer as Burnham pitches debate on ’how politics needs to change’ – as it happened
stories.summary: The prime minister said he wouldn’t be setting out a timetable to stand down if Andy Burnham wins the Makerfield byelection Jakub Krupa writes the Guardian’s Europe live blog. The European Commission has been asked about its response to the renewed talk about Britain’s potential future attempt to rejoin the European Union – but did not take a bait. At this stage, there are discussions on closer cooperation in a number of areas, that’s where we are, and that’s what we are doing precisely in prepa

stories.title: WHO considers use of experimental vaccines as Ebola cases and deaths rise in DRC
stories.summary: WHO chief said he was ‘deeply concerned’ after at least 500 suspected Ebola cases and 130 deaths reported in outbreak of Bundibugyo strain ‘It’s heartbreaking’: panic in eastern DRC over return of Ebola Global health leaders are considering whether vaccines or medicines still in development could be used to fight Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, as the World Health Organization’s chief said he was deeply concerned by the outbreak’s speed and scale. Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus sai

stories.title: Rubio criticizes WHO’s Ebola response as US continues sweeping public health cuts
stories.summary: US secretary of state says WHO was ‘a little late’ in identifying deadly Ebola outbreak in the DRC and Uganda The US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, said on Tuesday that the World Health Organization (WHO) was “a little late” in identifying the deadly Ebola outbreak in the the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. On Tuesday, Rubio told reporters: “The lead is obviously going to be CDC [Centers for Disease Control] and the World Health Organization, which was a little late to identify th

stories.title: Hearing aids may cut noise by reading brain signals
stories.summary: In a crowded room, it can be impossible for hearing aid users to focus on what they want or need to hear. Research into auditory neurotechnology may enhance sounds and voices and reduce noise in real-time.

stories.title: Trump: I called off attack on Iran planned for Tuesday
stories.summary: The US president said he had been asked to do so by the leaders of Qatar, Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates as "serious negotiations are now taking place."

stories.title: What really holds China and Russia together
stories.summary: The relationship endures despite a power imbalance, with both sides recognising that it is too important to fail.

stories.title: Three people killed as two teenagers open fire at a mosque in San Diego
stories.summary: Police say the two suspects had exhibited behaviour indicating a “generalised hate rhetoric” towards Muslims. The attack is one of the most major to target the Californian city’s large Muslim community.

stories.title: Fast-moving wildfire spreads through Southern California hills
stories.summary: A fast-moving brush fire in Simi Valley, Ventura County, has forced evacuations and damaged homes as it spreads across more than 180 acres in California.

stories.title: Swatch fans left frustrated as stores stay closed amid watch frenzy
stories.summary: Dozens of shoppers gathered outside a Swatch store in Lyon hoping to buy a sought-after watch released with Audemars Piguet, only to find the shop closed following disturbances and stock shortages.

stories.title: Europol disrupts thousands of IRGC online accounts across 19 countries
stories.summary: Europol announced a major digital crackdown that led to the removal of thousands of online accounts linked to Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) and the suspension of the group's primary X account in the EU.

stories.title: Tehran mass wedding turns military vehicles into bridal cars
stories.summary: Tehran stages a mass wedding where brides and grooms ride in decorated military vehicles, mixing celebration, symbolism and state messaging.

stories.title: Selling children to survive: Afghan fathers forced to make impossible choices
stories.summary: In Afghanistan today, a staggering three in four people cannot meet their basic needs.

stories.title: Victim or enabler? Epstein girlfriend who could face questions despite plea deal
stories.summary: Nadia Marcinko is relatively unknown but may soon be called before US legislators.

stories.title: Iranian TV teaches viewers to shoot as IRGC ramps up child recruitment
stories.summary: Iranian state TV has broadcast live weapons training, including simulated shooting at Trump, Netanyahu and the UAE flag, as the IRGC formally recruits children as young as 12 for checkpoints.

stories.title: Maldives cave diving tragedy: Search continues for missing Italian divers
stories.summary: Search and recovery operations remained underway in the Maldives’ Vaavu Atoll after five Italian divers went missing during a deep cave dive near Alimathaa Island.

stories.title: Thailand cuts visa-free stay period for more than 90 countries including UK
stories.summary: Many visitors who have been exempt for 60 days will soon have to apply for a visa after 30 days.

stories.title: Giant Pele statue installed outside stadium ahead of 2026 World Cup
stories.summary: A giant bronze statue of Pelé was installed outside Estadio Jalisco in Guadalajara ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

stories.title: G7 finance ministers gather in Paris amid Iran tensions and tariff concerns
stories.summary: Finance ministers from G7 economies gathered in Paris for a second day of talks focused on global economic pressures, including the Iran conflict, US tariffs and disruptions linked to the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: 'Ebola has tortured us': Fear grips eastern DR Congo as deadly virus spreads
stories.summary: The health minister has acknowledged that medics are playing catch-up with the virus after being slow to detect it.
